architectureNotes: Text is an ELEMENT (widget name 'Text', dataType 'paragraph'), NOT an assessment widget. 'paragraph' is absent from support/module-setup.ts ASSESSMENT_DATA_TYPES, so resolveDropTarget routes it to the canvas — it drops onto the Instructions-for-Students page (#desktop_view) via cy.dropComponent('Text'), not into a Generic Step column.
Main editable body is .paragraph-container.editables (contenteditable, ng-model fieldData.header) — set via cy.setContentEditableOn, never cy.type().
Settings panel id is #text-settings-panel with title 'Text' and icon .icon-Text_Paragraph.settingIcon.

Styles are data-driven from style_tab.stylesHolder; each <stylepanel> button is wrapped in .stylebgdiv[id] where id is the source style name: defaultText (Default, active by default), blockTextParagraph (Block Text), blockquote (Quote), dropCapflag (Drop Cap), emphasisText (Emphasis), buttontext (Button Text), buttonBorderText. Style 'applied' visual rendering is verified in Preview/Reader (deferred); presence/clickability is automated.
Settings actions: Upload Image (#inline-image), Insert Text (insertInlineText), Apply to all / Reset changes buttons, Show Label Type, Show Paragraph Number, Show Author Name (blockquote only).

Rich-text formatting (bold/italic/underline/align/math/special-char) lives in the floating textEditor.html toolbar (.editing-container .editor-list), mounted on focus. The spec degrades gracefully (log + return) when the overlay is not mounted in headless context.

QC TC_01–TC_09 are login/activity/step bootstrap (handled by the harness, not the Text element); TC_11 needs an Offline Step type; file pickers, math render, excessive-line-break limits, Preview/Reader/Publish, and persistence-after-reload are deferred.
